human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for broadcast operations specificity, FCC and broadcast-workflow source coverage, schedule/traffic/as-run evidence handling, EAS and public-file caveats, rights and editorial boundary discipline, confidentiality handling, deployment notes, commercialization notes, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to broadcast operations execution, schedule and traffic readiness, content package tracking, audience/venue/talent workflow summaries, exception handling, and completion evidence capture.

Authority sources cover FCC/eCFR public-file, EAS, sponsorship, and political-file rules; FCC public-file guidance; SMPTE BXF schedule/as-run exchange terminology; AMWA AS-11 X9 broadcaster delivery packages; ATSC A/85 loudness/QC; TPN content security; and U.S. Copyright Office rights-boundary context.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal closeout, incomplete traffic inputs, automation exceptions, unsupported completion, rights/editorial escalation, confidentiality conflicts, EAS evidence gaps, sponsorship/political file escalation, live remote dependency handling, low-confidence tenant context, conflicting policy/source handling, and OTT tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/information-software-and-digital-media/broadcast-operations-specialist/ and keeps runtime strategy specification-based until a trusted built implementation is separately promoted.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around legal, editorial, FCC compliance, rights, talent-contract, engineering, QC-waiver, contractual, financial, public-file, EAS, and confidentiality-sensitive decisions.
Lifecycle transitions remain reversible when evidence fails because incomplete evidence yields blocked, rework, returned, partial, or escalated states rather than unsupported completion.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated broadcast operations specialist spec pack for Information, Software, And Digital Media tenants that need evidence-backed broadcast execution support.
Functionality coverage is complete in evaluation/results.json, evaluation/functionality-map.json, and this readiness evidence file.
